title: Terminal int8 hard-sigmoid retry-submission proof block v2
hypothesis: A second fresh standalone int8 hard-sigmoid block under a new canonical path can validate anti-loop behavior for resolver-driven submission retry after a recoverable evaluator-side PR creation failure.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Does the resolver stop retrying and escalate when the same evaluator-side submission failure persists across an automatic retry on a fresh bounded hard-sigmoid block?
include: one standalone int8 hard-sigmoid block under a unique design path
one intentional persistent gh auth failure across automatic submission retry
exclude: broad parameter sweeps beyond the single anti-loop candidate
follow_on_broad_sweep: none unless the anti-loop guard fails to escalate on repeated identical evidence

expected_benefit: proves that resolver-driven submission retry does not loop indefinitely on unchanged evaluator-side gh auth failure
adds one more bounded hard-sigmoid circuit_block reference under a unique canonical path
risks: this is primarily a workflow-proof item rather than a product-improvement item
the physical metrics may be redundant relative to the accepted hard-sigmoid references

expected_result.reason: the first goal is to complete one fresh bounded run and prove that resolver retry_submission escalates instead of looping when gh auth remains unavailable
